summary_one_line: Font carrying both a 'morx' AAT table and a 'GPOS' OpenType table, so plan->apply_morx && plan->apply_gpos both hold.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font blob whose sfnt table directory simultaneously contains a 'morx' (AAT morph) table tag and a 'GPOS' positioning table tag, so the shape plan sets both apply_morx and apply_gpos true and the line-931 AND condition takes its true side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winning vpc seeds embed the exact 4-byte table-tag literals: seed 1 carries the OTTO sfnt magic plus the ASCII 'morx' tag at offset 0x0c, and seed 2 carries the ASCII 'GPOS' tag at offset 0x0c. Presenting both tags makes both apply_morx and apply_gpos plan flags true, so line 931 evaluates true and line 932 (hb_aat_layout_remove_deleted_glyphs) executes — the W-only resolving region.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds carry no 'morx'/'GPOS'/'OTTO' tag literals at those offsets (e.g. seed 1 holds ASCII 'y-hant-hk', seed 4 holds repeated 0x4c 0x0e dwords); without the matching 4-byte tags the plan flags stay false and line 931 takes only its false side (loser hit_div=0, branch never reaching the true side over all 10 trials).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP buckets: cmplog records the 4-byte table-tag comparisons (sfnt tag fourcc equality checks against 'morx'/'GPOS') and I2SRandReplace splices the captured constants directly into the input at the comparison offset, manufacturing both tags in one step. value_profile has only the Hamming/prefix-distance coverage feedback and no I2SRandReplace, so it must stumble onto the exact 4-byte fourcc by chance — which it never does over 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the AND condition requiring both apply_morx and apply_gpos to be true, with the true side globally blocked.
The true side of the blocker guards hb_aat_layout_remove_deleted_glyphs, which only the winner-resolving seeds reach.
A winning vpc seed contains the OTTO sfnt magic and the ASCII 'morx' table tag.
A second winning vpc seed contains the ASCII 'GPOS' table tag.
A loser value_profile seed carries unrelated ASCII content (a BCP-47-like 'y-hant-hk' string) and no font table tag.
The loser never accumulates any hits on the branch while the winner does, giving hit divergence of 46.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=46  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks the I2SRandReplace mutator and relies only on havoc plus token stack.
value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator on top of the havoc and token stack.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5728 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed ae73a8c9233c3cb8` and observed an I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only) at depth 18 of the 19-level ancestor chain; the dash-row floor is the I2S signal under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on apply_morx && apply_gpos where the table tags are required, in which value_profile resolves the true side as often as value_profile_cmplog within the same trial budget (showing the 4-byte fourcc match is reachable without I2S substitution).

weakest_evidence_point: The chain only shows one I2S-floor dash row at depth 18 (not at the immediate parent), so the exact 'morx'/'GPOS' fourcc substitution step is inferred from the byte presence rather than read directly off a labeled I2SRandReplace lineage entry.
